tasks.title: MongoDB Q&A inventory query
tasks.notes: 165 sources (64 HTML, 101 PDFs). 1,506 estimated Q&A pairs from HTML. GO signal met.
acceptance_criteria: Script queries ema_scraper.web_items for accordion Q&A pages and Q&A-pattern PDF URLs
Outputs scripts/phase0_inventory.csv with columns: url, type (html/pdf), topic_path, q_count_estimate, last_updated, revision_number
Counts printed: total sources, html vs pdf split, overlap with human-regulatory URL tree
Script is reproducible from a fresh MongoDB connection

tasks.title: Topic stratification + cross-ref chain completeness analysis
tasks.notes: 11 clusters. Research & Dev largest (492 Q&As, HTML+PDF). Chain completeness 43.6% from HTML links. 2 thin clusters (PDF-only).
acceptance_criteria: Identifies ≥3 distinct topic clusters from URL-derived topic_path grouping
Reports Q&A count per cluster; flags clusters with <5 Q&As as thin
Extracts cross-reference pattern ('see Q&A N') counts from PDF sources
Verifies what fraction of referenced Q&As exist in the same corpus (chain completeness %)
Outputs scripts/phase0_topic_report.md with all counts and a chain-completeness flag

tasks.title: Go/no-go decision notebook
tasks.owner_note: Claude generates; User makes the call
tasks.notes: Decision: GO. 'The project scope is clear. The clusters are representative and the chain completeness is ok.'
acceptance_criteria: Jupyter notebook scripts/phase0_scope_decision.ipynb with plots: source counts, topic split, chain completeness
Notebook has a clearly labelled 'Decision' cell that the user fills in: GO / NO-GO + rationale
Notebook committed with decision filled in before Phase 1 begins

tasks.title: Project setup — pyproject.toml, directory structure, schema doc
tasks.notes: All dirs created. pytest/ruff/mypy pass. Deps pinned at latest stable.
acceptance_criteria: pyproject.toml with pinned deps: pymupdf4llm, pymongo, rank-bm25, faiss-cpu, sentence-transformers, anthropic, pytest, ruff, mypy
Directory tree created: corpus/, benchmark/, harness/configs/, harness/judges/, harness/prompts/, results/, ablations/A/, ablations/B/, ablations/C/, docs/
corpus/SCHEMA.md documents Q&A record schema with all fields from ROADMAP Phase 1.1
benchmark/SCHEMA.md documents benchmark item schema with paraphrases field added
pytest/ruff/mypy all pass on empty stubs

tasks.title: HTML accordion extractor
tasks.notes: 10 tests pass. Capture rate 100% on 3 fixtures (33 items). Confidence logic based on ?-ending and interrogative words.
acceptance_criteria: corpus/extractors/html_extractor.py reuses/wraps existing ema_parser.py::_parse_accordion
Splits accordion items into Q/A pairs; flags items where heading isn't question-form
Builds topic_path from URL segments; assigns extraction_confidence
Output: list of QA records matching corpus/SCHEMA.md
Tests in tests/test_html_extractor.py: ≥3 real accordion pages used as fixtures; ≥90% Q&A capture rate on known pages
ruff + mypy clean

tasks.title: PDF Q&A extractor
tasks.notes: 14 tests pass. Benzyl alcohol PDF: 5 Q&As extracted, EMA/CHMP/508188/2013 reference correct. Cross-refs resolved to qa_ids.
acceptance_criteria: corpus/extractors/pdf_extractor.py uses PyMuPDF4LLM for text extraction
Regex-based Q segmentation on numbered headings ('1. Should the risk…', '2. What is…')
Revision history table parsed → revision field; reference number extracted from title page
cross_refs extracted from 'see Q&A N' patterns → list of qa_id strings
Flags PDFs without numbered-heading structure for manual review
Tests in tests/test_pdf_extractor.py: nitrosamine Q&A PDF as fixture; Q22 has correct cross_refs [Q20, Q10]; revision parsed correctly
ruff + mypy clean

tasks.title: build_corpus.py — pure dedup/filter/write logic (no MongoDB)
tasks.notes: Source-agnostic core: accepts Iterable[QARecord] from any caller. MongoDB adaptor wired in TASK-007. Testable entirely on fixture data. 16 tests pass; ruff+mypy clean.
acceptance_criteria: corpus/build_corpus.py exposes build_corpus(records: Iterable[QARecord], output_path: Path) → CorpusStats
Hash-based dedup on normalised question text; prefers PDF version when duplicate found; dedup_log written
Landing-page filter: drops records where extraction_confidence=='low' AND cross_refs==[] AND len(answer)<100; filter_log written
Stable qa_id = sha256(source_url + NUL + normalised_question)[:16] — already implemented in extractors, re-used here
CorpusStats dataclass: total_records, deduped, filtered, by_source_type, by_topic_path
Tests in tests/test_build_corpus.py: dedup drops HTML duplicate when PDF exists; landing page dropped; stats correct
ruff + mypy clean; no pymongo import in build_corpus.py

tasks.title: Mini-corpus HTTP fetcher — ~100 real Q&As without MongoDB
tasks.notes: Development fixture so Phases 3+ (embed, retrieve) can be built and tested before MongoDB is available. 10 curated EMA URLs; httpx with 1s delay; mini_corpus.jsonl gitignored. Not a substitute for full TASK-008 corpus.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/fetch_mini_corpus.py reads scripts/phase0_inventory.csv and fetches 10 curated HTML URLs via httpx (with polite 1s delay)
Each fetched page passed through html_extractor.extract_from_html(); records collected
build_corpus.build_corpus() called on collected records → writes corpus/mini_corpus.jsonl
Mini-corpus covers ≥3 distinct topic_paths and ≥80 Q&A records (or raises a warning if fewer fetched)
Script is idempotent: re-running overwrites mini_corpus.jsonl; HTTP errors on individual pages are logged and skipped (not fatal)
corpus/mini_corpus.jsonl added to .gitignore (large generated file)
Requires only: httpx (already a transitive dep or added to dev extras)

tasks.title: Deduplication + landing page filter + corpus writer (MongoDB driver)
tasks.notes: Thin MongoDB adaptor on top of TASK-007A. Blocked until MongoDB is available. build_corpus.py logic already done in TASK-007A.
acceptance_criteria: corpus/sources/mongo_source.py: records_from_mongodb(host, db, collection, query_filter) → Iterator[QARecord]
Calls html_extractor / pdf_extractor per document type; yields QARecord objects
build_corpus.build_corpus() called with mongo iterator → produces corpus/corpus.jsonl
Dedup log and filter log written alongside corpus.jsonl
Integration test (requires live MongoDB): connects, extracts ≥10 records, dedup runs cleanly

tasks.title: Corpus manifest — corpus.jsonl + corpus_stats.md
acceptance_criteria: corpus/corpus.jsonl written; one record per line; schema validates against corpus/SCHEMA.md
corpus/corpus_stats.md: counts by topic_path, source_type, revision date distribution, cross_refs coverage
Success criterion met: ≥200 Q&A records, ≥3 topic paths covered
If <200 records: script prints explicit SCOPE-RISK warning and halts

tasks.title: Training data contamination check — Dolma 3 / Common Corpus
acceptance_criteria: docs/training_data_verification.md written
5-10 distinctive sentences extracted from each main source doc (nitrosamine Q&A, level-of-detail Q&A, Quality Q&A parts 1/2)
Each sentence searched in Dolma 3 public release and Common Corpus
Per-source-document status recorded: present / absent / partial
OLMo 3 usability as clean reference documented per source

tasks.title: T1 Lookup benchmark questions — candidates + SME review
tasks.owner_note: Claude generates 30 candidates; User selects and validates 20
acceptance_criteria: Claude generates benchmark/candidates/t1_candidates.jsonl with 30 T1 candidates sampled from corpus
Each candidate has: question (verbatim Q from corpus), gold_answer, gold_qa_ids, topic_path
LLM generates 2 paraphrases per candidate; stored in paraphrases field
User selects 20 items, validates gold_answer accuracy, marks accepted=true in candidates file
20 final T1 items written to benchmark/benchmark.jsonl

tasks.title: T2 Scoping benchmark questions — author
tasks.owner_note: User authors; Claude validates schema and generates paraphrases
acceptance_criteria: User authors 10 T2 questions where the answer requires correct selection among topically-adjacent Q&As
Each question paired with: 2 topically-adjacent 'distractor' Q&As (wrong scope) + 1 correct Q&A
Questions stored in benchmark/candidates/t2_authored.md (human-readable) then converted to JSONL
Claude adds paraphrases (2 per item) and validates schema
10 T2 items appended to benchmark/benchmark.jsonl

tasks.title: T3 Multi-hop benchmark questions — chain composition
tasks.owner_note: Claude identifies valid chains; User composes the questions
acceptance_criteria: Claude enumerates all cross_ref chains of length ≥2 where all hops are in-corpus
Claude generates a chain map: benchmark/candidates/t3_chain_map.md showing each chain with hop Q&As
User composes 10 questions that require traversing those chains to answer (chain cannot be answered from first hop alone)
Claude validates all hops are in corpus and adds paraphrases
10 T3 items appended to benchmark/benchmark.jsonl

tasks.title: T4 Synthesis benchmark questions — hand-curate
tasks.owner_note: User hand-curates; Claude assists with paraphrases and schema
acceptance_criteria: User curates ≥5 synthesis questions combining ≥2 Q&As from different source documents
Includes ≥2 'composite/counterfactual' items that combine published Q&As in ways the documents don't (contamination-resistant by construction)
Claude adds paraphrases and validates gold_qa_ids span ≥2 distinct source_urls
≥5 T4 items appended to benchmark/benchmark.jsonl

tasks.title: Benchmark JSONL finalisation + validation script
acceptance_criteria: benchmark/validate_benchmark.py validates each item against benchmark/SCHEMA.md
Checks: no duplicate bench_ids; all gold_qa_ids exist in corpus.jsonl; paraphrases field present; type distribution T1=20, T2=10, T3=10, T4≥5
benchmark/STATS.md: stratification report, topic distribution, source diversity
Script exits non-zero on any validation failure

tasks.title: Closed-book contamination screen
acceptance_criteria: harness/contamination_screen.py runs every benchmark item through configured models with no retrieval context
Captures: model answer, matches_gold (bool), zero_shot_known flag per model
Slot-guessing test on 10-item subsample: masks numeric values, asks model to fill in; records hit rate
Results written to results/contamination/<model>_closed_book.jsonl
contamination_summary.md: aggregate zero_shot_known rate per model; which items are most exposed
PASS/FAIL gate: if zero_shot_known > 40% on T1 items for any model, those items are flagged REMOVE and must be replaced before TASK-021; threshold is configurable and documented in the script header

tasks.title: LlamaIndex VectorStoreIndex + FAISS backend
tasks.notes: Implemented as VectorStoreIndex (not DocumentSummaryIndex — no LLM required for indexing). cross_refs stored in excluded_embed_metadata_keys so only Q+A text drives embeddings. 11 tests pass incl. top-1 self-retrieval. Production run must re-index from corpus.jsonl after TASK-008 completes.
acceptance_criteria: harness/embed.py builds a LlamaIndex DocumentSummaryIndex from corpus.jsonl
Each Q&A record becomes a TextNode; cross_refs field becomes NodeRelationship.RELATED links between nodes
Embeddings use BGE-large-en via llama-index-embeddings-huggingface (wraps existing sentence-transformers dep)
FAISS flat index used as the vector store backend (llama-index-vector-stores-faiss)
Index persisted to harness/index/ — reloadable without rebuilding
Dense retrieval returns top-k (qa_id, score) pairs; unit test: known question retrieves itself in top-1
Document-level summary nodes generated for each source document (cheap, uses cheap LLM or extractive summary)

tasks.title: IDMP ontology concept tagging — node metadata
acceptance_criteria: scripts/tag_concepts.py parses IDMP RDF (from ~/Nextcloud/Datasets/) and extracts ~50-100 key regulatory concepts
Each Q&A node in the index gets a metadata['concepts'] list from simple string matching against the concept labels
Concept list written to harness/ontology/concepts.yaml for human review and editing
filter_by_concept(concept) retriever built on top of LlamaIndex metadata filters
Graceful fallback if RDF file is absent (concepts field left empty — index still works)
tasks.notes: Lightweight ontology-as-metadata — no graph DB. filter_by_concept() is the backend for A2 topic filter in TASK-023 (concept-based variant). Foundation for v2+ graph-RAG if benchmark demands it.

tasks.title: LlamaIndex BM25Retriever + hybrid RRF fusion
acceptance_criteria: harness/retrieve.py wraps LlamaIndex BM25Retriever (backed by rank-bm25) for keyword retrieval
QueryFusionRetriever combines dense VectorIndex retrieval and BM25 via Reciprocal Rank Fusion
Retriever mode selectable via config flag: dense-only (A0), BM25-only, hybrid RRF (A0+)
Test: hybrid outperforms dense-only on an exact-match query ('26.5 ng/day')
Both retrievers return (qa_id, score, node_metadata) — metadata includes concepts and topic_path
tasks.notes: BM25Retriever + RRF fusion implemented. QueryFusionRetriever avoided (requires OpenAI); RRF implemented manually. 8 tests pass.

tasks.title: Evaluation harness — retrieval metrics (Recall@k, Precision@k, Citation Accuracy)
acceptance_criteria: harness/eval_retrieval.py computes Recall@k, Precision@k, Citation Accuracy per benchmark item
All three metrics broken down by question type (T1/T2/T3/T4)
Output: JSON results dict + grouped bar chart PNG
Unit tests with synthetic gold qa_ids and retrieval lists

tasks.title: LLM judge — Faithfulness + Correctness
acceptance_criteria: harness/judges/faithfulness.md and correctness.md: judge prompts as files (not string literals)
harness/judge.py runs judge model (different from generator) on each answer
Judge returns score 0-1 + one-line rationale per item
Agreement validation: judge scores correlate >0.7 with hand-graded 20% sample (or flag for review)
Judge model configurable via config YAML

tasks.title: Config-as-code + results logging infrastructure
acceptance_criteria: harness/run_eval.py: single entry point, driven by YAML config
Config schema covers: retriever mode, generator model, judge model, k, ablation flags
Arize Phoenix launched at startup (px.launch_app()); LlamaIndexInstrumentor().instrument() called before first query
Each run creates results/<run_id>/ with: config.yaml copy, raw outputs JSONL, metrics JSON, plots, traces.jsonl
run_id = datetime + config hash (reproducible)
traces.jsonl: Phoenix span export per query (retrieval steps, reranking, LLM calls, latency)
Example configs in harness/configs/: baseline_a0.yaml, baseline_a0plus.yaml

tasks.title: Baseline run (A0 + A0+) and results report
acceptance_criteria: A0 (dense-only) and A0+ (hybrid) both run end-to-end on full benchmark
Results report results/baseline/baseline_report.md: all 5 metrics per T1-T4; open-book + closed-book side by side; lift computed
Contamination context: items flagged zero_shot_known excluded in a sensitivity analysis column
Report committed; baseline is now the fixed reference for all ablations

tasks.title: SME acronym dictionary — author
tasks.owner_note: User authors YAML; Claude integrates into A1 query expansion
tasks.notes: Unblocked after corpus (TASK-008) — SME knows EMA vocabulary without needing baseline results. Can be authored in parallel with Phase 2–3. Baseline results (TASK-021) are informative but not required.
acceptance_criteria: ablations/A_evidence_filter/acronym_dict.yaml written by user
≥30 entries covering: AI (Acceptable Intake), MAH, CAPA, ICH Q3A/M7/Q9, GMP, CEP, ASMF, TTC, LoQ, ppm/ppb, and any others encountered during benchmark curation
Each entry has: canonical, acronym, synonyms[], context_disambiguation[], topic_paths_where_relevant[]
Validated by Claude: no duplicate acronyms; context_disambiguation present for all collision-risk entries (AI, MA)

tasks.title: A1 query expansion + A2 topic-path filter implementation
acceptance_criteria: harness/ablations/a1_query_expansion.py: reads acronym_dict.yaml, expands queries contextually
harness/ablations/a2_topic_filter.py: two modes — (a) topic_path keyword filter, (b) IDMP concept metadata filter via filter_by_concept() from TASK-016.5; mode selectable in config
Both integrated as flags in run_eval.py config
Test: query 'What is the AI for nitrosamines?' expands to include 'Acceptable Intake'
Test: concept filter mode retrieves only nodes tagged with 'nitrosamine' concept

tasks.title: SME relevance rubric — author
tasks.owner_note: User writes ~200-word rubric for A3 LLM reranker
tasks.notes: Unblocked after corpus (TASK-008) — rubric is based on EMA Q&A domain knowledge, not benchmark outcomes. Can be authored in parallel with Phase 2–3. Baseline results (TASK-021) are informative but not required.
acceptance_criteria: harness/prompts/relevance_rubric_sme.md written by user
Rubric defines: what 'relevant' means for EMA Q&A (procedure match, scope alignment, threshold specificity)
Specifies non-relevant patterns: same keywords but different procedural step; wrong scope (MAH vs applicant, CAP vs NAP, biological vs chemical)
Version tagged as v1 in the file header

tasks.title: A3/A4 LLM reranker (SME rubric vs generic)
acceptance_criteria: harness/ablations/a3_reranker.py: LLM reranker using harness/prompts/relevance_rubric_sme.md
harness/ablations/a4_reranker.py: same architecture but generic 'is this relevant?' prompt
Both use Haiku-tier model to control cost; configurable
Cost budget enforced: ≤40 questions × 5 chunks × configurable max_rerank_calls
Integrated as flags in run_eval.py

tasks.title: Run Ablation A variants A0–A5 + analysis report
acceptance_criteria: All 6 variants run (A0, A0+, A1, A2, A3, A4, A5) via harness/configs/ablation_a_*.yaml
Results in results/ablation_a/: metrics JSON per variant, grouped bar chart (per T-type × variant)
A3 vs A4 comparison explicitly reported: is SME rubric authorship what helps?
ablations/A_evidence_filter/FINDINGS.md written: which variant gained most, on which types, pre-registered predictions vs actual

tasks.title: LlamaIndex ReActAgent + tools (search, follow_cross_refs, filter_by_topic, answer)
acceptance_criteria: harness/agents/react_agent.py builds a LlamaIndex ReActAgent with four FunctionTool wrappers
Tools: search(query,k), follow_cross_refs(qa_id), filter_by_topic(topic), answer(text,cited_qa_ids)
follow_cross_refs uses NodeRelationship.RELATED links from TASK-016 — O(1) lookup, no retrieval needed
Retriever mode uses best-A variant from TASK-026 (configurable in YAML)
Agent loop managed by LlamaIndex (no custom ReAct implementation)
Trajectories automatically captured by Phoenix instrumentation from TASK-020 — no extra logging code
Agent terminates on answer() call or max_steps exceeded; no infinite loops

tasks.title: Query cache — FAISS index over past query embeddings + JSON sidecar
acceptance_criteria: harness/query_cache.py builds and persists a secondary FAISS index over past query embeddings to harness/index/query_cache.faiss
JSON sidecar harness/index/query_cache.json maps each vector id to {run_id, question_text, answer_summary, rating, cited_qa_ids}
get_similar(query_vec, k, min_rating, threshold) returns ranked list of past interactions above cosine threshold
add_entry(run_id, question, answer, cited_qa_ids) appends to index + sidecar
update_rating(run_id, rating) patches the sidecar entry
Index reloads without rebuilding; handles empty-index case gracefully
Similarity threshold configurable (default 0.88); benchmark configs always set cache: false
tasks.notes: FAISS flat-IP index with L2-normalized vectors (cosine sim). 10 tests pass. ruff+mypy clean.

tasks.title: Semantic cache CLI — similarity lookup + user confirmation before pipeline runs
acceptance_criteria: Before each interactive agent run, query_cache.get_similar() is called
If matches found, CLI presents numbered list: similarity score, rating, question preview, answer preview, run_id
User options: [a/b/...] use cached answer, [c] use as few-shot context + run fresh, [Enter/d] run full pipeline
Cached answer path: returns stored answer + prints stored trajectory; no LLM call made
Fresh-retrieval path (options c and d): proceeds to agent; option c passes cached trajectory to TASK-027.7
run_eval.py respects cache: false in config — skips cache lookup entirely for benchmark runs

tasks.title: Runtime few-shot injection — top-k rated trajectories injected into agent prompt
acceptance_criteria: get_fewshot_context(query_vec, k=3, min_rating=4) fetches top-k similar trajectories rated >= min_rating from query cache
Fetches full trajectory text from Phoenix API by run_id (span export endpoint)
Formats trajectories as few-shot block injected into ReActAgent system prompt before each run
Injected examples are logged as metadata on the Phoenix trace (visible in UI, fully reproducible)
No injection if fewer than min_examples rated interactions exist (configurable, default 3)
k and min_rating configurable in YAML; injection disabled when cache: false
tasks.notes: No new dependencies. Phoenix SDK already in arize-phoenix dep.

tasks.title: CLI rating UI + Phoenix annotation posting
acceptance_criteria: After each agent run, CLI prompts: rate 1-5 (Enter to skip), optional note, optional per-step labels (good/suboptimal/wrong)
Rating posted to Phoenix via annotation API: {span_id, label='user_rating', score=rating, explanation=note}
Per-step labels posted as child annotations on step spans if provided
query_cache.update_rating(run_id, rating) called after Phoenix post so sidecar stays in sync
Rating step is always skippable (non-blocking); benchmark runs skip it automatically

tasks.title: JSONL export from Phoenix rated traces — feeds TASK-029 + reproducibility
acceptance_criteria: export_rated_traces(min_rating, output_path) fetches annotated traces from Phoenix API, writes JSONL
Each record: {question, trajectory, answer, cited_qa_ids, rating, step_ratings, run_id, model, retriever}
Running export on B1 traces produces ablations/B_process_rewards/trajectory_labels.jsonl (same schema as TASK-029)
Export can filter by min_rating, date range, model, retriever
Exported file is the reproducibility artifact — can be committed to git to snapshot a labeling session
tasks.notes: Unifies the online rating system with TASK-029 SME labeling — no separate labeling workflow needed for Ablation B.

tasks.title: B1 sanity check — 5 questions + trajectory review
tasks.owner_note: Claude runs B1 on 5 questions; User reviews trajectory quality and makes go/no-go call on B3 SME labeling
acceptance_criteria: B1 run on 5 benchmark questions (1 T1, 1 T2, 2 T3, 1 T4); trajectories saved
User reviews trajectories for: coherent thought steps, appropriate tool selection, no runaway loops
Go/no-go decision for B3 labeling documented in ablations/B_process_rewards/SANITY_CHECK.md
If NO-GO: TASK-029 skipped; proceed directly with B4 (tool descriptions only)

tasks.title: SME trajectory labeling (conditional on B1 passing)
tasks.owner_note: User labels trajectory steps; skipped if B1 sanity check fails
acceptance_criteria: User runs B1 on a held-out subset and labels ≥50 trajectory steps
Labels: good_step | suboptimal_step | wrong_step + one-line reason
Stored in ablations/B_process_rewards/trajectory_labels.jsonl
First 10 labels reviewed for consistency before continuing (label rubric may need iteration)

tasks.title: Run Ablation B variants B0–B4 + analysis report
tasks.notes: TASK-029 is conditional: if SANITY_CHECK.md in TASK-028 records a NO-GO for B3 labeling, TASK-029 is skipped and TASK-030 proceeds directly after TASK-028. Check SANITY_CHECK.md to determine which B variants apply.
acceptance_criteria: Read ablations/B_process_rewards/SANITY_CHECK.md first to determine which variants apply
If B3 GO: run B0/B1/B2/B3/B4; if B3 NO-GO: run B0/B1/B2/B4 only (skip B3)
Results in results/ablation_b/: per-type Recall@5, Correctness, Citation Accuracy + tool-call trace counts
B2 vs B3 comparison explicit if B3 ran (LLM-judge process reward vs SME labels); note if skipped and why
ablations/B_process_rewards/FINDINGS.md: pre-registered predictions vs actual; T3 multi-hop gain highlighted

tasks.title: SME few-shot exemplars — author
tasks.owner_note: User writes 3-5 Q&A solving traces; held-out from benchmark
acceptance_criteria: harness/prompts/few_shot_examples.md written by user
3-5 examples covering T1, T2, T3 question types (T4 optional if feasible)
Each example: question + retrieved Q&As + SME reasoning trace + answer with citations
Examples use Q&As from corpus that are NOT in benchmark (held-out)
Same examples used across all three model tiers (identical prompt)

tasks.title: OLMo 3 API + three model tiers setup
acceptance_criteria: harness/models.py: unified model interface for mid-tier (Haiku 4.5), frontier reasoning (Opus 4.x), OLMo 3 (Together AI or self-hosted)
OLMo 3 32B Think accessible via configured API endpoint
All three models testable via a single smoke-test call
Model versions pinned and documented in harness/configs/models.yaml
tasks.notes: Anthropic (Haiku/Opus) + Together AI (OLMo 3) via unified call_model(). 15 tests pass. TOGETHER_API_KEY required for OLMo smoke test.

tasks.title: 3×3 grid runs + analysis report
acceptance_criteria: All 9 cells run: 3 models × {zero-shot, SME few-shot, self-generated CoT}
Results in results/ablation_c/: per-type Correctness and Citation Accuracy for all 9 cells
Δ(few-shot − zero-shot) computed across all three model tiers — row-pattern consistency chart
OlmoTrace used on 5 OLMo 3 answers to verify contamination status post-hoc
ablations/C_prompting_matrix/FINDINGS.md: all pre-registered predictions checked; open questions highlighted

tasks.title: Blog post draft
tasks.owner_note: Claude drafts from BLOG_OUTLINE.md; User revises
acceptance_criteria: docs/blog_post.md follows structure in project_roadmap/BLOG_OUTLINE.md
All three ablation findings reported; 'surprising finding' section filled in from actual results
Contamination caveats section present
User has reviewed and approved final draft

tasks.title: README, final repo structure, and release
acceptance_criteria: README.md follows structure in project_roadmap/README_OUTLINE.md
Quickstart: pip install + run baseline + see numbers works from fresh clone in ≤30 min
Honest limitations section: corpus size, English-only, EU-only, no biomedical reasoning
CC-BY-4.0 (data) + MIT (code) licensing documented
EMA attribution section present

framework_decisions.rationale: DocumentSummaryIndex for document-tree memory, NodeRelationship for cross-refs, ReActAgent for agents, OpenInference+Phoenix for model-agnostic tracing. See .claude/work/2026-05-15_04_agentic-memory-architecture/exploration.md
